reviews.text: Truly terrible customer service, particularly their payments team who are completely incompetent and have taken 3 unauthorised payments from me for one month of storage charge. They will hound you via email when they want something from you but when you need to speak to someone to rectify an issue they will ignore you. There are much more professional clinics in London where you can get fertility treatment and receive much better care. I wish I had used one of them and not had the experience I have had with LWC.

The success rate of this clinic for my age, 40yo, is literally 0! But they would never tell you that. They don’t  work on weekends, and can easily mistime procedures. Plus after doing all the scans, if doesn’t look right and really …

I paid £570 upfront for tests at London Women’s Clinic and was later told I needed NHS clearance to proceed — something they never mentioned before payment. I was referred to an NHS gynaecologist who told me polyps often resolve on their …

I had a highly negative & disappointing experience with the London Women's Clinic Harley Street during my egg freezing cycle in July 2023. I was under the supposed "guidance" of Dr Tatiana Vasquez, yet I only saw her twice in person (for …

I had a hugely disappointing experience at this clinic. I went in for a consultation and ultrasound in mid-January, where I was told I might have a suspected unicornuate uterus. I was reassured I could still proceed with egg freezing and …
I have had 2 terrible experiences at this clinic, and so wanted to share so other women avoid them. …

Update:

I found the clinic through doing research and trusted them because of this and the fact that they are on Harley Street and you’d expect a better service from a clinic on “Harley street”

The biggest mistake I made was going to this clinic. On the initial consultation i told the nurse Georgina that I was adamant doing the procedure in March, because this was the only month I could do it( I travel a lot for work). However, I had a business trip when I was meant to get my period and mentioned to Georgina I would need to delay the period in order to start the process when I returned. I was told this was no problem and that I can take period blockers which would delay my period and I could start the egg freezing process when I was back.

I was given a prescription for norethisterone and told to take it once a day. This is what
I did - I was also told by a friend that she was on the same pills and was told to take 3 times a day, which confused me and I called the clinic and asked my coordinator Orla to confirm how many times a day I should be taking the medication, to which she said once a day.

Unfortunately, on the 24th of February I got my period, and when I checked the box of the medication it said I was meant to be taking the medication 3 times a day. When I called the clinic up I had to wait to then find out that they made a mistake and I was meant to be taking 2-3 pills a day. As you can imagine I was very upset and reminded them that this was the only month I could have done this procedure. At this point, I was put in contact with a doctor from the clinic, who ensured me that I could still go ahead with the egg freezing, even though my period started. The nurse said that she did treatment on people that don’t even get a period or patients that have cancer. Being compared to a patient with cancer doesn’t
Make me feel good. I am healthy and young I should not be compared to a patient with cancer.

To cut the story short, I could not do the treatment when I wanted to, as it was too late. I wasted money and time to attend all my appointments to then be misinformed about how to take medication. You would expect a fertility clinic to know the correct dosage. Do not compare your patients to others, educate your employees and stop taking advantage. Do not choose this clinic. They do not know what they are doing. All they care about is money, the communication is terrible. They are more expensive than other clinics. They will leave you in the dark and they only time you will have good communication is when they are taking payment from you.

They say they care about mental health but they do not, I am now not able to do the egg freezing until the end of the year, as I do not have the time. I made this all very clear when I first contacted the clinic. I was told “sorry it is human error”. I do not expect this human error from a “leading” woman’s clinic in Harley Street. This clinic chargers more than others, I would advice not choosing this clinic if you want to be taken care of, look elsewhere, into a smaller a smaller clinic that value their patients more. At this clinic you are just a number, they want your money and they don’t even know the ins and outs of treatment. I ended up having to google how many times a day I should be taking medication instead of being informed from either the nurse or my co ordinator.

I hope no other woman will have to go through what I went through.

I am writing to formally lodge a complaint regarding one of your staff members, Miss Khadeejah (please note that the spelling of her name may not be entirely accurate). I have just experienced one of the rudest, most unprofessional, and least helpful phone conversations I have encountered in recent years. I feel compelled to bring this to your attention due to the seriousness of the matter. Below are the main issues I encountered:
1：Call Termination:
While our conversation was still ongoing and I was trying to send her screenshots showing the issues I was having with filling out the form, she abruptly hung up the phone. This was not due to a dropped call or technical issue—it was clear she intentionally ended the call.:
2：Lack of Willingness to Help:
When I asked if my previous appointment could be retained, she bluntly told me it could not. However, when I later spoke to another one of your staff members, Rebekah, she was extremely helpful and professional and was able to retain my previous appointment. It was clear that Khadeejah simply did not want to assist me and was unwilling even to check the system on my behalf.
3：Refusal to Help with Rebooking:
After informing me that my previous appointment had been cancelled, I asked Khadeejah to help me rebook a new one. She refused, stating that I must first complete the “Change of Details” form. However, I was experiencing technical issues editing the form and needed support. Instead of offering any help, she insisted she could not assist and refused to help me secure a new date.
The above are just a few of the issues from our call, not the entirety of the conversation. Throughout the call, her tone and attitude were consistently unfriendly and dismissive. I sincerely hope your organization can investigate this incident. If your calls are recorded, I urge you to review the recording of our conversation.
Please understand that I have no intention to defame Miss Khadeejah—I do not know her personally, and we have no prior history. However, her conduct today was shockingly unprofessional and left us at LWC extremely disappointed. This is especially disheartening given that I am a returning client; in fact, we had some tests done at your clinic as far back as 2018.
I hope you take this complaint seriously and take appropriate steps to ensure no other clients are treated in the same way.

Words can't express the regret I feel having made the choice to work with this clinic and place my trust in them. Having moved on and shared my experience with other professionals I am validated by their universal support and agreement, that serious missteps were taken while I was a patient.

I went through two rounds of IVF with LWC. The nurses at the Brentwood clinic and at Harley Street were nice. Unfortunately Dr Kovats at the Brentwood clinic has no bedside manner, I found her to be rude, she actually laughed at me down the phone once and told me to lose weight. I asked to not speak to her following that incident. Following the second round of IVF, which failed, I was not contacted for approx two weeks. Only then to receive an email to state, sorry the doctor has only just informed us what happened. In that time the app kept sending me lovely reminder messages to take my medication for the embryo that I didn’t have. The fact that they can’t even communicate between themselves shows how disorganised they are. The after care is shocking. Don’t waste your time and money with LWC, I wish I hadn’t. There is a serious issue with the duty of care to their patients, considering the vulnerability of people going through fertility issues and the large amounts of money they are happy to take off you. I once cried while talking to one of the nurses as I wasn’t getting very far with follicle growth, next time I spoke to the doctor, she made me feel bad about that and suggested counselling, she said she would send me the details and never did. I’m disappointed and disgusted with how I have been treated. You just feel like a number being dealt with. Good luck and go elsewhere!

DO NOT use the London Women's Clinic! We are absolutely disgusted with them. They lost our personal information (ID documents and blood test results); they called my partner by the wrong name in person and via email (how can we be sure this mix up of names didn’t happen in the laboratory?); they have lied in our notes about what was said in meeetings; they failed to provide medical information, until our 3rd complaint; they continued to request tests at the very last minute (probably so we didn't have a chance to have them completed elsewhere); they requested over £3,000 payment for tests while I was at a scan, in reception (we had never been informed that this was to be paid up front as we didn't know until the day of transfer if it was needed), then continued to chase me, again in reception, at every scan; calls were not returned, emails not responded to, the list goes on. If it was just 1 thing, we probably would have laughed it off. But it was just 1 thing after another. Their organisation, empathy and professionalism is appalling. Just please, if you want a stress free treatment, avoid them. I don't want anyone to have to go through what we went through, still are 7 months later. They failed us entirely. The theme in the complaint responses was "we apologise for any distress caused, but..." followed by an excuse. Is that really an apology? "It was an oversight". These "oversights" should not continue to happen, surely? This has truly upset and angered us. The just don't care. It was all completely unnecessary and could have been avoided. This review doesn't even warrant a 1* as it suggests there was some level of service. There was not. No doubt any response to this review will be full of yet more lies. There's nothing more that can be said or done to put any of this right, the mental and physical damage has been done. It's broken our hearts. I do just hope that we have been the only 1s to suffer like this as I wouldn't wish this on anyone.

UPDATED: LWC did NOT complete a "thorough" investigation. In fact HFEA requested that LWC review the complaint because they hadn't responded fully. I did not disclose in my review that I had reported it to HFEA, so they're sharing information (now publicly) that is not theirs to share. However, for further clarification, HFEA responded to say "unlike some other healthcare bodies, we do not have a specific statutory duty to investigate patient complaints". Not really a "thorough" investigation is it?

I had hoped that our experience was not the norm (as explained in my review, I would not wish this on anyone) but it seems that we are not the only ones to have suffered, with much worse things happening. It has now come to light that they hide/delete/block any negative feedback because it "may cause distress to other patients". We felt we had done our due diligence in finding the right clinic for us - checking reviews etc. If the experiences that are now coming to light were not hidden by the clinic, we would never, ever have put our trust in them.

We did/do not have any specific "needs" that needed to be accommodated other than finding a clinic that cares about their patients/treats them with dignity and respect, which I would expect every patient has.

It has taken me some time to post this but after being chased ,a few years after the event, to sign forms for embryo destruction, I am once again reminded how poor their care was.

The consultant at the time didn't really answer complex questions. Everything was simplified, ie My stats/AMH etc were good, it was all good etc.

No one advised me of alternatives, range of opinion, risks and when i questioned why we were continuing as the stats did in fact not stack up, i was ignored and simply told to continue as it all looked fine. Not the case at all sadly.

I was also held to ransom when they realised I had been given the wrong costings advice. I had to pay the difference or else they would not release my embryos/test them.
In the end, I complained. Got a partial refund (try negotiating when you are without your medical notes/grieving) as I decided I didn't have the strength to take them to court.

I obtained my notes after the event. Zero oversight by a consultant during the process was noted in the notes. It confirmed my suspicion that it was very much a conveyor belt approach.

I know it will all depend what people's circumstances are, who your consultant is. All i will say is that we switched clinic and it was a completely different story. Just have your wits about you and make sure you have everything in writing.

I would describe LWC as a poor, substandard and lazy medical institution. The comments below are my honestly held, personal opinions based on my experience having IVF at the LWC in 2023. I could not go into the detail I wanted due to limited word count unfortunately.

I am 34 and had an admittedly difficult diagnosis of very low AMH, which was weaponised to repeatedly tell me that IVF would be a hopeless endeavour and strongly push towards [the much more expensive] egg donation route. The IVF protocol I was put on was incredibly basic - I felt there was a lack of any personalised treatment which could have better served me and my diagnosis.

There is no routine monitoring of blood work at all throughout an IVF cycle at LWC; this is truly baffling when considering IVF is a hormonal based treatment and the only way to assess its efficacy is to consistently monitor blood hormone levels. I found this to be lazy and an excuse to do the bare minimum.

I had one dominant follicle which burst. LWC brushed this off as "bad luck" as opposed to considering the impact of the extremely high stimulation I was placed on coupled with the lack of any proper monitoring blood work. In a meeting just after this news was broken to me, I was treated insensitively; I felt the doctor used this cycle failure to justify his own prediction of a poor outcome, as opposed to reflecting as to how a better outcome could be achieved in the future. I found this to be highly disappointing and again, lazy. I was again pushed to switch to egg donation.

I was then ignored and forgotten about - literally. I was promised a revised treatment plan and I heard nothing back. After 25 days, I contacted LWC to ask what was happening. I had simply been forgotten. This behaviour does not strike me as a clinic who truly cares about its patients. When the treatment plan arrived, it was extremely basic and contained no information as to how it would remedy the previous failures. I found this to be lazy and highly indicative of poor patient care.

The administrative side of LWC was chaotic; it adds rather than detracts from the stress you are under. Letters were sent after many weeks and only after chasing - I felt they were curt, poorly written and contained limited helpful medical advice. Paperwork was regularly not in order.

I felt something was not right, the doctor had no faith in me, he seemed unmotivated in helping me achieve a successful outcome - seemingly contented with his diagnosis of me being bound to fail. The doctor also strongly suggested that no other clinic would be able to achieve success either, telling me verbatim: "if anyone tells you that they can collect more than one egg from you they are lying".

Despite the LWC's indication that I was a hopeless case which no fertility clinic could help, I decided to seek a second opinion. After 2 rounds of IVF at another clinic, we made 6 embryos and I am now 4.5 months pregnant. The LWC's dogmatic and rigid assessment that I would was an unsuitable candidate for IVF was simply medically incorrect. Had I stayed at LWC, I would have never had the chance to have my own children - and they sought to convince me this was an inevitability.

I formally complained to the LWC; they made partial admissions to "poor communication" but nothing further. My complaint was poorly handled, with many points I raised being ignored. Before I escalated the complaint to the regulator, I provided the LWC with a further chance to reply more fully as a courtesy - they declined. This struck me as a business who did not care what their patients thought of them, nor what the regulator would think of them.

LWC not only took our hard earned life savings, but something much, much more valuable - our time. I felt denigrated and unsupported during one of the most vulnerable times of my life.

Please, please carefully consider whether this clinic is for you - particularly if you have a more complex infertility diagnosis. You deserve to have the best treatment possible during what is probably one of the most difficult times of your life.

So far the communication from this place has been abysmal. Our coordinator Maria takes days to respond (if at all) and multiple chases are required to get an answer. Owing to her tardiness, we have likely missed the opportunity to start treatment this cycle meaning a whole month of delays. Further, the costs quoted are approx double what we were expecting for a single cycle (over £14k) and although I requested a call last Wednesday to discuss, this has yet to happen. We had originally picked LWC from the three that we paid for consultations with (even though they were by far the most expensive) because we assumed this would gain us a much better level of service. This absolutely has not been the case and as we have already missed the window for this cycle, we are reassessing whether to trust LWC with such an important part of our lives at all. Avoid.

I had a really negative experience with this clinic. I chose London Women's Clinic due to their supposed 'human-centered approach' which sounds great to attract customers but I found the reality was the opposite of human-centered. Consultants and nurses gave me inaccurate information during consultations and appointments.  I felt abandoned during my treatment. I believe this clinic is deliberately using unethical practices.

PS: reposting so the clinic can identify my name and treatment date in their records.

Awful experience in 2020 with this clinic.
Only seen a doctor once when I was being signed-up. My treatment and all my scans were done by nurses. We haven't had a follow-up call until weeks after my end of treatment (only when we called to request it). I had to request my treatment summary and document myself instead of being given an official document at the end of the treatment. Despite the poor outcome, the doctor advised I enroll again and follow the same protocol (we did not obviously). Every extra scan, blood test etc was charged, the patient is never given any refund. It's heartbreaking to see this clinic take advantage of the vulnerability and despair of some women/couples.

Bad experience very expensive and dont bother to get back to you, you will constantly be chasing them. They do everything to get extra money instead of focusing on patient care. Cannot recommend I would suggest going with a smaller clinic for a more caring approach. We know see a lovely little clinic and it's half the price of this place and they offer much better care. Never fear of the bill at the end with my new clinic.

I had a bad experience in the darlington clinic recently my treatment didn't work I paid over £10k, I over stimulated the clinic did not care I felt bloated and in pain. There is no after care serivce not even a phone call to ask how am I coping, they only care about making money not making lives.
I would ring the emergency nobody would answer when they would I get told there are certain times, so how is this classed as an emergency helpline for patients.
Overall London womens is poor do your home work research read reviews dont be made a fool thinking they will help you.

Absolutely appalling/terrible service from this clinic. Can they be trusted? Sometimes you wonder with the level of organisation they have, if they could actually arrange a proverbial in a brewerey. …

I can honestly say i wouldn't recommend the essex clinic. A doctor who didn't introduce herself as a doctor was more then rude and needs to be a litle more choice in ther wording while undertaking an ultrasound. I was spoken to by another …

The initial process of getting the appointment and having the 1st appointment for myself and partner was fine staff was friendly everything was okay. Now on the day i was suppose get my consultation  i spoke to one of the doctors and i must …

I really didnt want to post a negative post but unfortunately my experience was awful here. From the start it was a problem from filling in the forma that the doctor didnt even discuss with me, never being able to contact anyone. …

Awful experience - they literally lead us down the garden path with a treatment that was not suitable at all. Having transferred to another clinic who have given us clear statistics about our situation, we would never have pursued the route …
Avoid like the plague! Absolutely awful place. I actually don’t know where to start as so many things went wrong & the attitude from the staff is horrible. …

Myself and my partner went here to get initial tests and consultation done. I couldn't understand the terms used by the doctor in the following consultation by phone (I have a doctorate in psychology). We were left on our own from there to have follow-on scans organised (this is explained in a lengthy letter emailed by a non-personal account). I concur with the opinions of other low-scoring Reviewers. Overall, I don't recommend this clinic.

I undertook an altruistic journey of egg donation at that clinic,to start with -trusting and enthusiastic, which I later on realised - naive. Egg collection is not as easy as advertised.When I started the treatment I was told to give myself …
I would tend to agree with the other reviews about lack of organisation, communication and empathy. …

A terrible experience, I would NOT recommend. 1 star given to my allocated care coordinator Francesca who was absolutely brilliant throughout the entire journey. The rest of the clinical team and service were extremely poor. I plan to make a complaint.

I would not recommend the Harley St branch of this clinic for those with suspected endometriosis. We were treated as unexplained infertility even though an endometrioma was found on my wife's ovary. The consultant said this did not mean …

AVOID THIS CLINIC!! Please please please take the time to research clinics and read through the many 1 star reviews this company has received, you will notice common complaints and they are true, from experience I am sad to say. …

London Women's Clinic - Brentwood: Very disappointing. Only after we went to another fertility clinic in London, did we realise just how bad our experience was. At our first consultation to review our test results, the consultant kept …

I had one natural cycle of IUI at this clinic. The service here was awful:
1. Clinic does not open at weekends so if your cycle occurs then there is no chance to get pregnant! Oh sorry it opens till 12 on Saturday. still not ideal …

The payment was so unclear. It was given by the nurse on a piece of paper and she kept adding and deleting costs so both of us got very confused. Although we paid £500 for the initial consultation/tests, I decided not to proceed with this …

I always paid my invoices on site for whenever we went to LWC  Harley Street (reference number M0176315), however received a letter from debt collection for an amount due of £210 which is a joke! Very unprofessional setup for chasing clients for invoices which have been already paid and causing harassment.

We visited this place for our ivf treatment and had two attempts.
In both cases not only did zero eggs make it to fertilisation but even more worryingly my wife ended up in a&e with accute pain. This happened like clockwork …

Terrible customer service. Impossible to get in contact with. Called and emailed for 3 days after my wife's initial appointment but no response. Have booked elsewhere on Harley Street. Avoid. (Response to below comment: yes I am certain I have the correct clinic)

Unorganized and no cooperation between people. Everything you agreed and confirmed with one person another asked about again.
They also didn't choose best quality embryo for transfer despite available ones, which resulted in early miscarriage.

Terrible service. Paid lots of money for the 'ovulation tracking scan' for it to turn out to be just one scan. Nothing was explained on the phone when I was booking it and when I explained how misleading the information is on their website they agreed but never took the information down and did not do anything to try and resolve the situation. Terrible misleading service, will never go back.

Wish i could give them 0 stars!! There is no customer care what so ever. Its all about the money!! Only ever had one call and thet was for payment. 2 days ago was my test date, habent heard a thing from them to see my result. It was all a shambles from the start with mistakes being made. Go else where!!

Money making scam! Doctors don't care about you and consider not what is best in your situation but how they can make more money out of you. The whole experience was a disaster so far. Thinking of switching to another clinic rather than proceeding.

Avoid at all costs, I have sent in an inquiry 2 weeks ago with no reply. I have since tried to call numerous times and every time I get through and explain that I am awaiting a reply, they shut the phone in my face. It’s so completely unprofessional. I haven’t even started with them and they are already avoiding my calls.
I am in the process of freezing my eggs and LWC is not a great option. They are one of the more expensive options and their attention to detail is really poor. I wouldn't recommend them.

Poor and frustrating. You meet doctors only when they have to sell you a service, then you are treated like in a production line...and the worse, negative results are on you, they won't take responsibilities. I can't believe that such a business is allowed in UK.
Not transparent with billing, load up cost in a very opaque way until you are at a point where it's not possible to change provider. Are not customer focused at all. Be very cautious
awful experience! the staff need to learn what compassion is! I would not recommend this place to anyone...I think kindness is a strange word for both doctors or nurses there

I was told an incorrect price for blood test. By the doctor/nurse I was told 50, but they charged me 105 when I arrived! And in whispers one of the receptionists said something which suggested I was trying it on! Rude!
Dreadful. Utterly abysmal from start to finish. From reception, to equipment to consultant, to crap advice. This place is AWFUL. AVOID AVOID AVOID!

Terrible experience. Had been to the Bridge Centre for consultations but transferred here for collection. Big disappointment when collection was missed since they booked me too late. Even though they expected to be paid.

Receptionist not wearing a mask, when London is in tier 3. Selfish and unlawful.

This was 112 Harley Street, Dec 18 AM
Worst clinic ..no scan before Insemination

Looking at the negative reviews, it’s shockingly sad to see that we had a very similar experience to many of them…

So it not “just bad luck” …
The worst clinic in the world!!!

Hi The City Gal, thank you for taking the time to review. We're sorry you felt your experience with us didn't meet expectations. We haven't been able to locate your patient file from this review but would love to speak to you and learn more … More

Hello Curisprit, thank you for taking the time to review. We're sorry that you feel a one star review represents your service. Unfortunately we cannot find your patient file from this review. If you could reach out to us directly, we'd be interested to hear your thoughts. Best wishes - the London Women's Clinic Harley Street Team
Hi Ans - we don't have any record of you at London Women's Clinic. Would you have been under a different name? If so, we'd love to contact you to find out more.

Hello JC, thank you for taking the time to review. We're sorry that you feel a one star review represents your service. If you could reach out to feedback@londonwomensclinic.com, we'd be interested to hear your thoughts.
Kind regards,
LWC Harley Street Team

If you’re looking for genuinely individualised IVF treatment, there are many better options in London that put patients first at the same cost. In our experience, LWC offered a fairly standard, one-size-fits-all approach at a premium price. …

My experience with Harley St… the staff are amazing ! Very compassionate ready to answer any questions to their full understanding of the circumstances-  I have gone through ivf 4 times with in the year without taking a break and they have …

Do not recommend.

I attended to begin egg freezing. When I wanted to start the treatment, I found …

My husband and I went for a fertility check. Everyone was very nice. The facilities also seemed nice. In the end I was very disappointed with the insensitivity of the doctor who delivered our results. He was pushing IVF even before we knew …

The overall experience is a bad. The first doctor i saw was very factual and gave me little chance of success. bluntly. I still went ahead. The nurses are a disaster. It was my first time and was scared of needles and had no one to talk to. They refused to help me with injections the first time and insisted i should go home and do it alone. I cried all the way home. The woman who does the scans is quite unprofessional. she is mostly in a bad mood and makes you feel uncomfortable. I told the nurse before the operation that no one can pick me up. cause i hadnt told anyone and i didnt live in London. On the operation day they refused to sedate me if no one will pick me up. basically they wanted to operate on me without anesthesia! I agreed, and they did a scan before the operation i had already ovulated. it was a heart break all the way through!
There is nothing particular positive to say about this clinic. You rarely see a doctor, most of the time nurses treat you. If you see or talk to a doctor it is never the same.

Very disappointed with my experience.

After going through two full cycles at this clinic, I feel increasingly let down rather than supported. What I expected was professional guidance, care, and a genuine willingness to help patients navigate such an important and emotional journey. Unfortunately, that was not what I experienced.

Instead, I often felt that the process was treated as routine rather than personalised. I did not feel there was enough responsibility taken or proactive effort to truly understand or improve my situation. Over time, this has not only been disappointing, but has also affected my confidence and trust — not just in this clinic, but more broadly in fertility services and even parts of the UK healthcare system.

For something as sensitive and significant as fertility treatment, patients need more than just procedures — they need care, attention, and a sense that the doctor is truly working with them to find solutions.

Sadly, I did not feel that here, and based on my experience, I would not recommend this clinic.

Hi Dety. To leave more detailed feedback of your experience in 2016, please email feedback@londonwomensclinic.com.
Kind regards,
LWC Harley Street Team
Although the individual staff at the hospital were all nice and kind, I found the overall organisation of my egg freezing process at London Women’s Clinic really quite poor and confusing. …

Not impressed by the standard of this clinic. I had a fertility MOT with them, 2 hostesses not even smiling or welcoming, looking down at me. I am reviewing this clinic 3 stars only because the doctor was excellent.
